Title: Loss of wbpL disrupts O-polysaccharide synthesis and impairs virulence of plant-associated Pseudomonas strains

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato DC3000; Pseudomonas cichorii ATCC10857/DSM50259
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato; Pseudomonas cichorii
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato DC3000; Pseudomonas cichorii ATCC10857/DSM50259
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato; Pseudomonas cichorii
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: O-specific antigen/common polysaccharide antigen initiation step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyses the initiating glycosyltransferase step required to start O-polysaccharide synthesis; in Pseudomonas aeruginosa it initiates both CPA and OSA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for synthesis of the terminal product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key initiating step for OPS synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: bacterial outer membrane as part of LPS
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: required for efficient host colonization, swarming motility and full virulence in planta
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes OPS synthesis; knockout causes OPS-deficient LPS, reduced swarming motility, reduced host entry/apoplastic amplification, and reduced virulence symptoms
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted gene knockout; plasmid complementation and cross-complementation; LPS extraction and silver-stained SDS-PAGE/NuPAGE; gel permeation chromatography; plant infection assays; swarming motility assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. wbpL orthologues were identified in plant-associated Pseudomonas genomes and are conserved at syntenic positions. 2. Deletion of wbpL in P. syringae pv. tomato DC3000 and P. cichorii abolished OPS production, demonstrating that WbpL is essential for OPS synthesis. 3. OPS-deficient ΔwbpL mutants showed reduced swarming motility and reduced virulence/host colonization. 4. Complementation restored OPS synthesis in P. cichorii but not detectably in P. syringae pv. tomato, while cross-complementation in P. cichorii supported conserved WbpL function.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Arabidopsis thaliana spray inoculation or pressure infiltration; lettuce spray and stab inoculation; in vitro swarming conditions on KB 0.4% agarose
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: gentamicin resistance cassette promoter (constitutive in complementation constructs)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: comparative genomics/BLASTP and synteny analysis; no accession-specific omics dataset reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss of OPS reduces motility and virulence; ectopic expression may only partially restore OPS and may alter OPS substitution frequency
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential antimicrobial target to attenuate virulence of plant-pathogenic Pseudomonas by disrupting OPS biosynthesis initiation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In P. syringae pv. tomato DC3000, plasmid-driven complementation did not restore OPS, likely due to expression dosage/context effects rather than loss of protein function; transient expression of Pst-wbpL complemented the P. cichorii mutant, supporting conserved function across species.
